title: pavonia elegans
intro: Pavonia elegans, also known as Rocky Mountain Pavonia or Rose Mallow, is a flowering plant species native to the western United States and Canada. It is a member of the Malvaceae family, characterized by its showy, pink to purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Pavonia elegans include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to support overall health and well-being, particularly in the treatment of skin conditions and respiratory infections.
